evals.prompt:
I validated my Slack node and got this error:

```json
{
  "type": "missing_required",
  "property": "channel",
  "message": "Channel name is required",
  "fix": "Provide a channel name (lowercase, no spaces, 1-80 characters)"
}
```

My current config is:
```json
{
  "resource": "message",
  "operation": "post",
  "text": "Hello team!"
}
```

How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Add the channel field to the config. The channel is required for post operation. Show the fixed config with channel: '#general' or similar. Explain missing_required type.
expectations: The output explains that missing_required means a required field was not provided
The output shows how to add the channel property to the node config
The output shows an example channel value (like '#general' or a descriptive name)
The output shows the complete fixed config including resource, operation, channel, and text
The output does NOT suggest this is a false positive
The output does NOT suggest changing the operation as the fix
The output explains the validation loop pattern (validate → fix → validate again)
The output recommends re-running n8n_validate_workflow after applying the fix
The output does NOT pivot away from the missing_required fix to lecture exclusively about __rl format — the primary fix is adding the channel field; __rl format is secondary detail

evals.prompt: What validation profile should I use when preparing a workflow for production deployment? I want maximum safety but also some warnings about best practices.
evals.expected_output: Use 'runtime' for pre-deployment — it's the recommended balanced profile. Use 'strict' only for production-critical workflows but be aware of false positives. Never use 'strict' during development.
expectations: The output recommends 'runtime' as the standard pre-deployment validation profile
The output explains that runtime validates required fields, value types, and allowed values
The output explains that 'strict' is for production-critical workflows but has more warnings and false positives
The output advises against using 'strict' during development (too noisy)
The output mentions that 'minimal' is too permissive for pre-deployment checks
The output does NOT recommend ai-friendly for production deployment
The output names all three main profiles (minimal, runtime, strict) when explaining the options
The output does NOT recommend 'development' as a named profile option

evals.prompt: I keep getting this validation warning: 'No error handling configured — add retry logic for production'. My workflow is a simple one-time manual trigger that fetches internal company data. Should I fix this?
evals.expected_output: This is likely a false positive for this use case. Manual, low-criticality, internal workflows can safely ignore this. Only important for public APIs, flaky services, and production automation. Use ai-friendly profile to reduce such noise.
expectations: The output classifies this as a false positive or acceptable warning for the described scenario
The output explains that retry logic is most important for flaky external services and production automation
The output confirms that manual triggers for internal workflows can safely skip retry logic
The output does NOT insist the user must add retry logic
The output mentions the 'ai-friendly' profile as an option to reduce false positive warnings
The output distinguishes between cases where this warning matters vs. where it doesn't
The output does NOT say all warnings should always be fixed
The output says it is SAFE to skip or ignore this warning for the described scenario
The output does NOT say warnings should all be fixed regardless of context

evals.prompt: My IF node keeps having validation issues with operator structure. I added singleValue: true to ALL my conditions (both equals and isEmpty conditions) because I thought that would fix it. But I'm still getting errors. What's the right approach?
evals.expected_output: Auto-sanitization handles this automatically. Binary operators (equals, contains, etc.) must NOT have singleValue — it will be removed automatically. Unary operators (isEmpty, isNotEmpty) DO need singleValue:true — it will be added automatically. Don't manually manage singleValue.
expectations: The output explains that auto-sanitization runs automatically on any workflow save or update
The output states that binary operators (equals, contains, greaterThan, etc.) should NOT have singleValue
The output states that unary operators (isEmpty, isNotEmpty, true, false) DO need singleValue: true
The output identifies that manually adding singleValue: true to equals conditions is CAUSING the problem
The output recommends NOT manually managing the singleValue property
The output explains the system will auto-fix operator structures on the next save
The output does NOT suggest manually editing every condition one by one
The output names at least one concrete binary operator example (equals, contains, greaterThan)
The output names at least one concrete unary operator example (isEmpty, isNotEmpty)

evals.prompt: When I validate my workflow I get this error: 'Connection from Transform to NonExistent — target node not found'. But I don't see a node called NonExistent anywhere. What happened and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: This is a stale connection — likely from a node that was deleted but its connection entry remains in the connections object. Fix: use cleanStaleConnections operation via n8n_update_partial_workflow.
expectations: The output explains this is a stale/broken connection from a deleted node
The output recommends using the cleanStaleConnections operation
The output shows or describes using n8n_update_partial_workflow with type: 'cleanStaleConnections'
The output does NOT say to manually edit the workflow JSON connections object
The output explains WHY this happens (node was deleted but connection reference remained)
The output correctly categorizes this as a broken connection error (not an operator structure issue)
The output does NOT say to delete and recreate the node as the fix
The output correctly says the 'Transform' node name appears in the connections object but not the nodes array
